title: Edinburgh 7-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Edinburgh 7-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Edinburgh, United Kingdom: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Edinburgh Castle and Palace of Holyroodhouse. Prices from $1441 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Edinburgh, United Kingdom — Cool temperate climate with cold, windy winters (highs around 7°C) and mild summers (highs around 19°C); weather shifts rapidly and rain is common in any season. May to September for mild weather and the Fringe Festival in August, or December for Christmas markets and Hogmanay. This itinerary blends Edinburgh Castle, Palace of Holyroodhouse, Royal Mile with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Edinburgh Castle, Palace of Holyroodhouse, Royal Mile
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Edinburgh
itinerary.description: Land at Edinburgh Airport (EDI) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Edinburgh Castle — Historic fortress atop an extinct volcano, home to the Crown Jewels. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Edinburgh: Palace of Holyroodhouse & Camera Obscura & World of Illusions
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Palace of Holyroodhouse — The King's official residence in Scotland. In the afternoon, continue to Camera Obscura & World of Illusions — Victorian optical-illusion attraction on the Royal Mile. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Edinburgh: The Real Mary King's Close & Scotch Whisky Experience
itinerary.description: Morning visit to The Real Mary King's Close — Guided tour of Edinburgh's hidden underground streets. In the afternoon, continue to Scotch Whisky Experience — Interactive whisky history tour and tasting. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Rosslyn Chapel & the Pentland Hills
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Rosslyn Chapel & the Pentland Hills, a popular side trip from Edinburgh. Return to Edinburgh in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Rosslyn Chapel & the Pentland Hills

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Dean Village — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Edinburgh: Edinburgh Dungeon & Royal Yacht Britannia
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Edinburgh Dungeon — Theatrical scare-tour through Edinburgh's darker history. In the afternoon, continue to Royal Yacht Britannia — Former royal yacht museum ship docked at Leith. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Gilmerton Cove or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to EDI for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Fringe Festival accommodation months ahead — prices spike and rooms sell out in August.
Buy Edinburgh Castle tickets online for a timed-entry slot.
The Royal Mile gets extremely crowded in August — side closes and Victoria Street offer quieter alternatives.
Arthur's Seat is free to climb but wear proper shoes as paths get slippery.
Many major museums and galleries are free entry — budget tour spend accordingly.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
A waterproof, windproof jacket for unpredictable weather
Layers to add or remove through the day
Comfortable shoes suited to steep cobbled streets
A warm hat and gloves for winter visits
thingsToDo: Hiking Arthur's Seat and Salisbury Crags
thingsToAvoid: Overpriced tartan and whisky shops directly on the Royal Mile.
Climbing Arthur's Seat in poor weather without proper footwear.
Assuming August accommodation will be available last-minute during the Fringe.
Street "characters" on the Royal Mile who expect a tip for photos.
Underestimating wind chill on Calton Hill and Arthur's Seat.
topRestaurants: The Witchery by the Castle — Fine Dining Scottish ($$$$)
The Shore Bar & Restaurant — Seafood & Scottish ($$$)
Deacon Brodie's Tavern — Traditional Pub Fare ($$)
instagramSpots: Edinburgh Castle from Princes Street Gardens
Dean Village along the Water of Leith
Calton Hill monuments at golden hour
adventureActivities: Hiking Arthur's Seat and Salisbury Crags
Coasteering day trips near North Berwick
Cycling the Water of Leith path
Climbing at Edinburgh's Alien Rock centre
luxuryExperiences: Private whisky tasting flight at a members' club
Stay at The Balmoral with Castle views
Private guided history tour of the Old and New Towns
Chauffeured day trip to the Scottish Highlands
honeymoonExperiences: Candlelit dinner at The Witchery by the Castle
Private Highlands day tour for two
budgetExperiences: Free entry to the National Museum of Scotland and Scottish National Gallery
Hiking Arthur's Seat for panoramic views
Wandering the Royal Mile and Grassmarket
Free Fringe Festival street performances in August

festivalCalendar.name: Edinburgh Festival Fringe
festivalCalendar.timing: August, the world's largest arts festival

localEtiquette: Queueing is strictly observed — never push in.
Tipping around 10% in sit-down restaurants is customary.
Scots are generally warm and informal; a friendly "cheers" or "hiya" is common.
Stay quiet and respectful at war memorials and inside Edinburgh Castle's chapels.
During the Fringe, street performers may seek attention — a polite decline is completely fine.
localTransportGuide: Edinburgh's compact Old and New Towns are best explored on foot, though hilly; Lothian Buses and a single tram line connect the airport, city centre and Leith affordably with a day ticket. The Royal Mile's cobblestones and closes are only accessible on foot.
bestTimeToVisit: May to September for mild weather and the Fringe Festival in August, or December for Christmas markets and Hogmanay.
weather: Cool temperate climate with cold, windy winters (highs around 7°C) and mild summers (highs around 19°C); weather shifts rapidly and rain is common in any season.
visaInformation: UK visa rules apply — US, Canadian, Australian, New Zealand and most EU passport holders can visit visa-free for up to 6 months as a Standard Visitor. Most visa-exempt nationalities (including EU citizens) now require an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) before arrival, while other nationalities such as Indian passport holders need a UK visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~10.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Edinburgh?
faqs.answer: UK visa rules apply — US, Canadian, Australian, New Zealand and most EU passport holders can visit visa-free for up to 6 months as a Standard Visitor. Most visa-exempt nationalities (including EU citizens) now require an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) before arrival, while other nationalities such as Indian passport holders need a UK visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Edinburgh?
faqs.answer: May to September for mild weather and the Fringe Festival in August, or December for Christmas markets and Hogmanay. Cool temperate climate with cold, windy winters (highs around 7°C) and mild summers (highs around 19°C); weather shifts rapidly and rain is common in any season.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Edinburgh safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Edinburgh is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Edinburgh?
faqs.answer: The local currency is GBP. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Sofia Ivanov
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Edinburgh Castle in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Chen Botha
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Palace of Holyroodhouse in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mohammed Müller
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Royal Yacht Britannia alone was worth the trip, but combined with Edinburgh Castle this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Wang
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Edinburgh Dungeon alone was worth the trip, but combined with Arthur's Seat this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Sarah Silva
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Edinburgh is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Princes Street Gardens. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: James Smith
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Our trip to Edinburgh was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Camera Obscura & World of Illusions was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Emma Kowalski
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Edinburgh was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Princes Street Gardens was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Tanaka
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Dean Village and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
